action: Pre-batch CLI version lifecycle (operator request 2026-06-13): regularly check claude/codex/agy versions and update them DELIBERATELY before the cycle pipeline starts — never mid-batch (the existing in-batch freeze stays; claude 2.1.173's mid-soak self-update killed 3 batches on 2026-06-11). Build in internal/looppreflight + a doctor subcommand: (1) VERSION INVENTORY: capture `claude --version`/`codex --version`/`agy --version` at every batch start into loop-preflight.json AND the run record (forensics: the 2.1.173 incident took hours to attribute because no batch recorded which CLI versions it ran). (2) DRIFT DETECTION: persist last-seen versions; if a FROZEN CLI's version changed since the previous batch, WARN loudly naming the freeze that leaked — live evidence: claude moved 2.1.173->2.1.175 between 2026-06-12 batches DESPITE settings.json autoUpdates:false (either the native freeze does not gate all update paths, or PATH resolves a different install than the frozen one — investigate as part of this item and extend the PR-#82 freeze recognizer accordingly). (3) DELIBERATE REFRESH PATH: `evolve doctor cli-refresh [--apply]` — check currency (brew outdated for codex; claude/agy native channels), apply updates ONLY at the batch boundary with operator opt-in or explicit flag, then RE-FREEZE (brew pin codex / autoUpdates:false claude), then VALIDATE before any batch may start: boot-smoke each driver (exists) PLUS a busy-chrome canary — drive one tiny generation per CLI and assert PaneBusy/Progressed fire and the pane does not go blank (the two 2.1.173 regression classes; the canary was already proposed in the processed claude-cli-version-freeze inbox item — implement it here). (4) POLICY: check every batch (cheap); update only when stale AND validated; a failed canary refuses the refresh and keeps the old binary. Wire the inventory+drift parts into the existing looppreflight accumulate-then-decide checks (stage them as WARN, not HALT, except a failed post-update canary which must HALT). TDD pins: drift detection fires on a synthetic 2.1.173->2.1.175 transition under a frozen marker; canary failure blocks batch start; inventory lands in loop-preflight.json and the run record. ROOT-CAUSE EVIDENCE (2026-06-13 00:1x investigation): ~/.local/bin/claude symlink flipped to versions/2.1.175 at Jun-12 18:14 — ONE MINUTE after soak #2 launched (18:13) and AFTER autoUpdates:false was already set; the native freeze does not gate the staged-update-on-boot path. PR #82's recognizer (settings autoUpdates:false => frozen) is therefore insufficient evidence of an effective freeze — the drift detector (component 2) is the only reliable guard; consider also pinning by symlink target hash or comparing `claude --version` against the version recorded at freeze time. Secondary: a second claude exists at /opt/homebrew/bin/claude (PATH-shadowed) — the freeze/inventory must name WHICH binary each driver resolves.
evidence_pointer: go/internal/looppreflight/ (freeze.go PR #82, checks.go); claude 2.1.173->2.1.175 drift despite autoUpdates:false (observed 2026-06-13 00:0x, batch w4soak3); 2026-06-11 three-killer incident (busy-chrome + blank-pane, memory: project_concurrency_factory_campaign); processed inbox claude-cli-version-freeze (busy-probe canary proposal)
